mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher during rate cuts (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 4.0 percent, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of triggered instances, or threshold crossing shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with declining nonfarm payrolls signals deteriorating labor market fundamentals, which reduces consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing output, which forecasts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during economic contraction phases.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances during recession regimes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no positive correlation with 20-day equity returns over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.2 million units, construction sector contraction and related supply chain disruptions trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 70% of triggered instances, or threshold crossing yields Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of falling interest rates and rising existing home sales indicates successful monetary transmission to the housing market, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction signal predicts negative returns in > 60% of cases, or joint Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rising above 5.0 percent during a financial stress period amplifies credit risk concerns, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of triggered instances during stress regimes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
